title: sporadanthus interruptus
intro: Sporadanthus interruptus, also known as Māpou, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, native to New Zealand. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties due to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sporadanthus interruptus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
